stories.text: As an application developer, I want to declare a channel as durable at creation time so that all messages published to it survive process restarts without changing my publish code.
As an application developer, I want to declare a channel as ephemeral so that I get zero-overhead message delivery with no persistence cost for transient data.

As an application developer, I want to choose between dedup and durable-conversation exactly-once strategies per channel so that simple consumers use the lightweight approach and complex consumers use the heavyweight one.
As an application developer, I want to configure the dedup TTL per channel so that idempotency key storage matches my expected retry window without unbounded growth.
As an application developer, I want to checkpoint my consumer cursor after processing a batch so that a crash only replays messages from the last checkpoint, not from the beginning.
As an application developer, I want to replay messages from a specific offset so that I can reprocess a range of messages without resetting my entire consumer state.
As an application developer, I want to provide a processing receipt after handling a dedup-protected message so that duplicate deliveries return my original result instead of re-executing.
As an application developer, I want my durable conversation to resume from the last completed step after a crash so that side effects from completed steps are never re-executed.
As the Aion engine, I want to dispatch workflow activities through durable channels so that activity assignments survive worker crashes and are retried without duplicate execution.
As the Aion engine, I want activity processing to use durable conversations so that multi-step activities resume from their last checkpoint on re-delivery rather than re-executing from scratch.
As the Aion engine, I want the durability layer to use haematite's append/cas API directly so that workflow state and channel state share the same storage engine without an additional dependency.
As an operator, I want to inspect a consumer's persisted cursor offset so that I can determine how far behind a consumer is relative to the partition head.
As an operator, I want crash recovery to be automatic on process restart so that durable channels, cursors, and conversations resume without manual intervention.
As an operator, I want to see the dedup cache size and TTL expiry rate so that I can tune the dedup window to balance memory usage against duplicate protection.
